patient.query: I think the hardest part has been the constant rejection. It feels like every application I send out is just... tossed aside. I start to wonder if I’m not good enough or if I’ll ever find something that fits me. It's just draining, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I'll never find something that fits me

patient.query: When I get a rejection, I can almost feel this weight just drop on my chest. It’s like my mind goes to all those negative places—am I not qualified enough? Did I mess something up in the interview? I start questioning my skills and whether I even belong in this field. It's just tough to shake off those thoughts.

do I even belong in this field?

patient.query: Honestly, it’s usually something like, “What’s wrong with me?” It feels like I’m just not doing enough or that everyone else has it figured out while I’m stuck. It’s so frustrating because I know I have so much to offer, but in those moments, it just doesn’t feel that way at all.

automatic_thoughts: Everyone else has it figured out

patient.query: I think it’s just this voice in my head that always seems to come out when things get tough. It points out all my flaws and mistakes, like a spotlight on my insecurities. There are times when I look at my achievements and I want to be proud, but then that voice just undermines it all, making me feel like I’m not really worthy of my successes. It's exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: not really worthy of my successes

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like it’s always saying things like, “You’re a failure,” or “You’ll never get hired.” Sometimes it even brings up things from my past, reminding me of times I messed up. It feels like this never-ending loop, and it makes me doubt everything, even the things I thought I was good at. It just amplifies my fear of not being enough, you know?

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. It’s like I’m constantly fighting an uphill battle, and some days, I just don’t have the energy for it. I want to be resilient and push through, but I often feel so overwhelmed. It’s hard to see a way out when the noise is so loud, and I just want to find some peace with it all.

patient.query: Definitely. I think it’s partly the expectations I put on myself. I feel like I need to be perfect, especially when it comes to my career. And then there’s the pressure from others—friends and family, who seem to have it all figured out. It’s like I’m always comparing myself to them, and I end up feeling even more lost. It just feels like I’m running a race where everyone else is miles ahead, and I’m stuck at the starting line.

automatic_thoughts: everyone else has it figured out

patient.query: I think a lot of it comes from my upbringing. My parents always had high expectations, and I was the one who was supposed to excel in everything. I internalized that, and now it’s like I carry this weight everywhere I go. I want to make them proud, but sometimes I feel like no matter what I do, it’s never enough. It feels like I’m chasing this ideal that’s just out of reach.

automatic_thoughts: I need to make them proud
I am chasing an unreachable ideal

patient.query: I remember always having to bring home top grades or win competitions, whether it was sports or academics. There was this constant pressure to be the best, and anything less felt like a failure. It was like my worth was tied to those achievements, and I didn’t feel like I could show any weakness or struggle. If I wasn’t excelling, I felt like I was letting everyone down, especially myself.
